title: verbena ribifolia var. foetida
intro: Verbena ribifolia var. foetida, also known as St. Joseph's tear or St. Joseph's weed, is a plant species in the verbena family that is native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Verbena ribifolia var. foetida include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antipyretic to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
